bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with the all-valueC bid, which gives me very low utility, and there is no evidence of concession yet. Since time is at the very start, I will target my maximum-utility bid and hold firm on all issues for now.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, and the opponent has repeated an all-valueC offer that is very low utility for me, with no evidence of concession yet. I will stay close to my maximum-utility position while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous all-valueA bid by making a small concession on a low-weight issue.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 rather than making major concessions. The opponent has repeated the all-valueC bid, which is very low for me and shows no real concession yet; I will make a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (~0.961) because it is still very early (t=0.06) and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering all valueC. Since there is no evidence of flexibility from them yet, I will only make a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding my highest-weight issues at valueA and avoiding repeating my previous exact bid.
